data.body:
Big Numbers Are Hard

It is both true that 4% increase in overall food insecurity, from 10-14%, represents ~14 million more people being food insecure

It also means that 86% - or ~301 million people, are not food insecure

Food insecurity remaining within a 5% band is consistent, not a radical rise
